<!-- generated by session_final_report for h5bench/20260710_061131 at 2026-07-11 00:22 UTC -->

## h5bench read+write baseline & optimization — final report

### Scope
Narrowed mid-session (user directive) to `read` and `write` workloads only. The other 5
workloads (append, overwrite, write_unlimited, exerciser, hdf5_iotest) were baselined earlier
in the session and left as-is, not further optimized.

### Bugs found and fixed
- **`read` was silently broken**: `h5bench_read` requires a pre-existing file matching its
  config's dims; it does not create one. The original scripts pointed it at a fresh empty
  path, producing an HDF5 error-storm that still exited 0 and produced a bogus trace. Fixed
  with a write-then-read two-phase script + `write_for_read.cfg`.
- **`write.cfg` was oversized 16x** (`DIM_1=33554432`, ~1.6-1.7TB/replicate, 35-40+ min/run
  measured via real `du` on the actual `.h5` file — trace-log size was a misleading proxy).
  Shrunk to `DIM_1=2097152` -> confirmed 575GB actual, 12.13min actual elapsed at 768 ranks.
- Cleaned ~510k loose, un-segregated stray trace files from a pre-folder-segregation-fix
  debris accumulation (confirmed junk, user-authorized deletion via `drm`).
- A runaway job-submission-loop incident (~3,200 queued jobs across 5 allocations, not
  caused by this agent) was caught and resolved by the coordinator mid-session; a new
  occupancy-check rule was added to the `flux-alloc` skill as a result.

### Optimization results (read+write only)
| Lever | Verdict | Result |
|---|---|---|
| meta_block_size (8MB) | Kept, zero-cost | Metadata-op win confirmed (POSIX `lxstat`/`lseek` significantly faster); aggregate-bandwidth effect inconclusive (noise-dominated at n=5) |
| Paged file-space strategy | REJECTED | Fundamental HDF5 1.14.x incompatibility with `COLLECTIVE_METADATA=YES` |
| **ROMIO cb_nodes=16 + CRAY_CB_NODES_MULTIPLIER=2 + lfs setstripe -c16 -S4M** | **CONFIRMED WIN** | **+490.5% median POSIX bandwidth, non-overlapping 5-rep ranges, ~8.7x faster wall time, comparator-corroborated (open/lseek/lxstat all significantly faster)** |
| Network: NIC policy + rendezvous threshold (combined) | INCONCLUSIVE | Median +38.6% but same-rep comparator showed +3.3%, negligible |
| Network: rendezvous threshold (isolated) | INCONCLUSIVE | Median +68.2% but same-rep comparator showed +3.0%, negligible |
| NUMA cpu-affinity=per-task | INCONCLUSIVE | Median +39.8% but same-rep comparator showed -0.5%, negligible; corroborates a prior no-effect finding on this same system for an unrelated workload |
| Huge pages / madvise | REJECTED | Implementation gap: `malloc()` buffer isn't page-aligned, `madvise(MADV_HUGEPAGE)` fails every call; system THP is otherwise available |

**Recommendation: adopt the ROMIO cb_nodes+striping config as the h5bench write default on
Tuolumne/lustre5.** Every other lever tested this pass was either rejected outright or
showed a misleadingly large 5-rep median delta that a same-rep `comparator` cross-check
revealed as noise — a methodological finding now recorded generically in
`dftracer-optimization-kb` (comparator corroboration required before any win claim).

### Data volumes and quota
Session workspace ~2.2GB (well under 500GB budget). Peak `/p/lustre5` usage during this
session's active work: ~12-13TB (well under the 20TB pipeline budget), all app-output
cleaned up via `drm` after each confirmed trace capture.
